# UC-13.6.15 · Grafana Loki Log Query Cardinality Explosion Detection

## Description

LogQL queries that explode into millions of streams degrade Loki queriers and front-ends, often triggered by unbounded label selectors after deployments. Surfacing heavy-query signatures from Grafana’s Loki client logs protects the shared logging tier.

## Value

Avoids querier OOM loops and keeps incident investigations fast when Loki backs Explore.

## Implementation

Prefer shipping Loki `query_stats` JSON if available; map `streams`, `chunks_downloaded`, `queue_time_seconds`. Coach teams to use bounded time ranges and recording rules for high-fanout filters.

## SPL

```spl
index=observability sourcetype="grafana:log" loki
| search "lines processed" OR "total entries" OR "post-filter" OR "chunk fetch"
| eval heavy=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)high cardinality|large query|max chunks|too many entries"),1,0)
| rex field=_raw "(?i)streams[=:](?<streams>\d+)"
| bin _time span=5m
| stats sum(heavy) as heavy_hits, median(streams) as med_streams, count as log_lines by _time, org_id
| where heavy_hits > 0 OR med_streams > 5000
| sort -heavy_hits
```

## Visualization

Timechart of heavy_hits, table of offending org_id, histogram of streams.

## References

- [Grafana Loki — Best practices](https://grafana.com/docs/loki/latest/get-started/best-practices/)
